text: Let's just hope that he's a man of his word. They seem to back up what they say. Some of them get in office and don't do anything. They deal with the reality more. (AE) No.
I think that by trying to cover all bases - social problems - it adds to our being in debt. The programs to help too many different groups - it costs us too much money.
It probably is a more open party. It would include more groups like Indians, all Blacks, social interest groups.
It has not been able to attract as broad a base, like women and minorities.

They stress individuals rather than groups. You don't see social types of programs. And free enterprise - they don't have as many restrictions ,e.g. aid to farmers not to grow - they would not be as enthusiastic.
overly socialistic

the last 8 years gas prices and cost of living has gone up,,harder for people like me to make a living
They seem to be more for the middle class working people.

Too much big business. Everything big business wants, they get. In union disputes, the companies are favored. They're manipulated by big business. Iaccoca, for example, knows how to manipulate government.
I tend to favor them more. I think there's more unity. The Dems. seem to stick together on policies and other things.
It seems as if they're having problems coming together. The budget was a problem.
they give real benefits to low income and middle class people.they may represent us more.
they do not pay much attention to low income and meiddle class.they do no have the sense about what we want.
